a new way to scale AI capabilities
lets models "think" during downtime
AI agents use "sleep" time to process information and form new connections by rewriting their memory state
can use their "sleep" time to process information and form new connections
models "think" before they answer

models that "think" before they answer
a recent reasoning model

trained to output large reasoning traces before returning a final response
a new paradigm introduced in a research paper
represents an exciting new scaling direction for stateful AI systems
activates deep thinking during idle periods
expands what AI systems understand and how they reason
allows machines to think while they sleep
agents run even while they “sleep”
uses downtime to reorganize information and reason through available information in advance
transforms “raw context” into “learned context”
introduces the sleep-time compute paradigm
can benefit from sleep-time compute for scaling
pushed beyond what's possible with active interactions only
should be running even while they “sleep”
use downtime to reorganize information and reason
exist in environments with persisted, re-used context
can study a codebase in advance
can reflect on previous interaction from the user to consolidate information
transformed into “learned context” during sleep time
can be used later during test time
reduces the need for additional reasoning later on
crucial for agents to learn and evolve over time

work began with a vision of agents that learn and evolve
fundamentally stateless
have no persistent memory of experiences or learnings
creates a critical bottleneck in how we can scale compute for AI systems

creates a Pareto improvement in model performance
shifts computational load from high-latency user interactions to periods when the system would otherwise sit idle
expected to have significant impact beyond math and coding domains
only benefit from increased computation during active use (test time)
reset between interactions

maintain memory (or context) over time

similar to Letta agents today

The field of AI is advancing very quickly.
Generative AI systems are opaque and differ fundamentally from traditional software.
Interpretability needs to mature quickly to matter.
This post makes the case for interpretability.
Generative AI systems are opaque.
Their internal mechanisms are emergent rather than directly designed.
They are grown more than they are built.
They consist of vast matrices of billions of numbers.
Traditional software programs do things because a human specifically programmed them in.
